name: Digest long emails with PostgreSQL and Gmail

options.systemPromptTemplate: You are a precise extraction engine. Only extract values present in the text.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO draft_log (draftId, topic, audience, logged_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());
SELECT * FROM drafts WHERE updated_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' ORDER BY updated_at DESC;

parameters.subject: =✅ Digest long emails with PostgreSQL and Gmail — run completed at {{ $now.toFor
parameters.message: =✅ Digest long emails with PostgreSQL and Gmail — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO draft_log (draftId, topic, audience, logged_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05722-draft`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Collect Raw Text

### Collect Raw Text

Shapes the item: writes `content`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Extract Structured Fields

### Extract Structured Fields

Pulls structured fields (draftId, topic, audience) out of free text using a strict extraction prompt.

Ports: ①→ All Required Fields Found? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Extract Structured Fields** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### All Required Fields Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.output?.draftId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Upsert into Google Sheets · ②→ Queue for Manual Review

### Upsert into Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Draft Tracker', matched on `draftId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Merge Review Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Queue for Manual Review

Posts a message to Slack — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Review Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Merge Review Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Enrich with Reference Data · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per draft (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Draft Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Gmail · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

# Digest long emails with PostgreSQL and Gmail

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Digest long emails with PostgreSQL and Gmail. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual blog posts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Collect Raw Text → Extract Structured Fields → All Required Fields Found? → Upsert into Google Sheets → Merge Review Paths → Serialize Run Output (CSV) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating blog posts in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; OpenRouter API key; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The required-fields check runs *before* the write, not after — an incomplete extraction goes to manual review instead of landing a half-populated record in the system of record. Upsert (not plain create) means re-processing the same document twice updates the existing record instead of duplicating it.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Add a new field to the extraction schema and mark it required — watch more documents route to manual review.
2. Feed it a document deliberately missing a required field and trace exactly where it ends up.
3. Change the extraction target (e.g. invoice → contract fields) and adjust the upsert key to match.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your blog posts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
